topic.what: Systematic review of Shen-Ling-Bai-Zhu-San (SLBZS) — composed of Ginseng (Ren Shen), Poria (Fu Ling), and Atractylodes (Bai Zhu) as principal herbs — for chronic gastritis presenting with spleen-qi deficiency pattern.
topic.how: Narrative and systematic synthesis of clinical studies, RCTs, and experimental investigations on SLBZS; outcome domains include symptom scores (bloating, fatigue, loose stools), endoscopic mucosal appearances, inflammatory biomarkers, and gut microbiota modulation.
topic.why_matters: Spleen-qi deficiency is the most prevalent TCM pattern in functional and chronic organic gastritis; SLBZS is a canonical spleen-tonic formula with a 1000-year clinical history, yet its modern evidence base and mechanism profile are poorly consolidated, limiting its evidence-based adoption.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Improvement in gastrointestinal symptoms associated with spleen-qi deficiency (bloating, fatigue, loose stools, poor appetite)
characteristics.value: Consistent symptom improvement reported across reviewed clinical studies; effect size moderate to strong in pattern-matched patients
characteristics.evidence: Synthesis of RCTs and observational studies reviewed
characteristics.context: Strongest effects in patients diagnosed with spleen-qi deficiency pattern; weaker in unselected gastritis populations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Changes in intestinal microbiome composition associated with SLBZS treatment
characteristics.value: Increased abundance of beneficial taxa (Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium); decreased Helicobacter and pro-inflammatory dysbiotic species in experimental and some clinical studies
characteristics.evidence: 16S rRNA sequencing data from included studies
characteristics.context: Microbiota data mostly from animal studies; human microbiome evidence limited

good_for: Clinicians managing chronic gastritis with functional-dyspepsia-like spleen-qi deficiency presentation
Researchers exploring prebiotic/probiotic-like mechanisms of polysaccharide-rich TCM formulas
KB articles on TCM pattern-matched treatment selection
not_for: Heat-pattern or damp-heat gastritis (formula is warming-tonic, contraindicated in heat excess)
Acute H. pylori eradication as primary endpoint
requires: TCM pattern differentiation competency for appropriate patient selection
Access to original RCTs for quantitative meta-analytic synthesis

novelty: Consolidates scattered clinical and experimental evidence for SLBZS under a unified pattern-matched evidence framework
Highlights gut microbiome modulation as a plausible mechanistic bridge between TCM spleen theory and modern gastroenterology
open_problems: Optimal dosage and preparation form (decoction vs. granule vs. tablet) for chronic gastritis not standardised
Lack of blinded RCTs with objective endoscopic and histological endpoints
Which constituent herbs (Ginseng, Poria, Atractylodes) carry most of the therapeutic burden is undetermined
